commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `late-dispatch-after-final` Telegram proof scenario across the Mantis prompt, Telegram dispatch QA fault injection, the Crabbox proof runner CLI, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +39, Tests +47, Docs +3, Other +31. Total +120 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows Mantis validates `CANDIDATE_SHA` as the open PR head, while the proof command is trusted and the SUT behavior comes from the candidate worktree. A candidate head that lacks this PR's Telegram env hook can accept the runner flag without injecting the fault in the candidate runtime.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can adjust the prompt/runner guard without changing the Telegram runtime fix itself.

target.title: Add Telegram late-dispatch proof scenario
target.body:
## Summary
- Add a private-QA Telegram proof scenario for a late dispatch/cleanup error after a visible final reply has already been delivered.
- Teach the Crabbox Telegram proof runner to accept `--proof-scenario late-dispatch-after-final` and pass the matching QA fault env into the local gateway.
- Update the Mantis Telegram Desktop proof prompt so duplicate generic-fallback PRs use this scenario for baseline and candidate runs.

Refs #89930
Refs #87299

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed:** Mantis now has a supported runner scenario for the Telegram duplicate generic-fallback proof class: a final reply is delivered first, then a private-QA late dispatch/cleanup error is forced.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, branch `proof-harness`, commit `c37099d1ec6fd1be1a7217b5678ccd846ace631a`. No live Telegram Desktop capture was run for this harness PR; the native proof run is the downstream consumer once this trusted harness lands.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node --import tsx scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts --help`
- **Evidence after fix:** Console output from the proof runner CLI after this patch:

  ```text
  Usage:
    node --import tsx scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts [probe] [--text /status] [--expect OpenClaw]
    node --import tsx scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts start [--tdlib-url <url>]
    node --import tsx scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts send --session <session.json> --text <text>
    node --import tsx scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts run --session <session.json> -- <remote command>
    node --import tsx scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts view --session <session.json> --message-id <id>
    node --import tsx scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts screenshot --session <session.json>
    node --import tsx scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts status --session <session.json>
    node --import tsx scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts finish --session <session.json>
    node --import tsx scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts publish --session <session.json> --pr <number>

  Useful options:
    --proof-scenario <name>        QA proof scenario. Supported: late-dispatch-after-final.
    --dry-run                     Validate local inputs and print the plan.
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The trusted proof runner advertises the new `--proof-scenario late-dispatch-after-final` CLI input that Mantis can append to baseline and candidate proof runs. The local tests also verify that this option is parsed and passed into the gateway as `OPENCLAW_TELEGRAM_QA_DISPATCH_FAULT=late-dispatch-after-final` while preserving the private QA flags.
- **What was not tested:** A live native Telegram Desktop before/after capture for #89930. This PR only adds the trusted proof scenario and prompt wiring; #89930 remains the behavior-fix PR that should consume it after this lands.

## Verification
- `node --import tsx scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts --help`
- `pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts test/scripts/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:check -- extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts test/scripts/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.scripts.json scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts test/scripts/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm build`

## Notes
- `pnpm check:changed` could not run locally because the configured Crabbox binary failed its basic `--version`/`--help` sanity check before executing repository checks, so I ran the targeted local checks above.
- This PR only adds the proof scenario and prompt wiring; the Telegram duplicate-fallback behavior fix remains in #89930.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
